meta.description: Fix patches for 11 items where confusables and common_errors_ja had duplication or misplacement

principle.confusables: 実在する別の表現との比較(意味の違い、register の違い、使い分け)
principle.common_errors_ja: 対象語自体の誤用パターン(時制・冠詞・前置詞・動詞誤用・語順)

notes: 各 item について confusables と common_errors_ja のみを差し替え、他のフィールドは変更しない
confusables には『英語では言わない』『誤用』『不自然』の記述は含めない(それらは common_errors 側)
confusables の各要素は example_en を持つ(実在する表現の使用例を示すため)
全 21 items のうち 11 items が対象(52%)、10 items は問題なし

confusables.similarity_ja: 写真を撮る意味で同じように使える
confusables.key_difference_ja: take a picture より take a photo の方が現代口語で頻出、意味は同じ
confusables.correct_usage_ja: どちらも正しく、場面で使い分ける必要はない
confusables.example_en: Let me take a photo of the sunset.

confusables.similarity_ja: 写真関連の take 表現で似ている
confusables.key_difference_ja: take a shot はカジュアルでスナップ的、take a picture はより一般的
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 写真家の話や砕けた会話では take a shot、通常は take a picture
confusables.example_en: The photographer took a shot of the runners at the finish line.

confusables.similarity_ja: picture を使う表現で語形が似ている
confusables.key_difference_ja: draw a picture は「絵を描く」で行為が違う、take a picture は「写真を撮る」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 絵を描くなら draw、写真を撮るなら take
confusables.example_en: The children drew a picture of their family.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I make a picture.
common_errors_ja.correct: I take a picture.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 写真を撮るときは make ではなく take を使う。「絵を作る」感覚で make としがちだが、写真は take

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I took picture of the sunset.
common_errors_ja.correct: I took a picture of the sunset.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 通常は不定冠詞 a が必要。数えられる名詞なので a picture が基本形

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I took a picture to my friend.
common_errors_ja.correct: I took a picture of my friend.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 被写体を示すときは to ではなく of を使う

confusables.similarity_ja: 朝食を食べる意味で同じ
confusables.key_difference_ja: eat は食べる動作、have は食事全体を経験する広い意味
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 動作を強調するなら eat、朝食の時間全体なら have
confusables.example_en: I don't have time to eat breakfast today.

confusables.similarity_ja: 朝食に関する対比表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: have は朝食を取る、skip は朝食を抜く(反対の行動)
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 食べる場合は have、抜く場合は skip
confusables.example_en: You shouldn't skip breakfast — it's important.

confusables.similarity_ja: 朝食に関する表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: make は朝食を用意する(調理)、have は食べる(消費)
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 作るなら make、食べるなら have
confusables.example_en: My father makes breakfast for the family on Sundays.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I make breakfast every morning.(食べる意味で)
common_errors_ja.correct: I have breakfast every morning.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 食事を「取る・食べる」意味では have を使う。make は「作る」なので調理の話になる

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I have a breakfast at 7.
common_errors_ja.correct: I have breakfast at 7.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 食事名の前に不定冠詞 a は付けない(breakfast / lunch / dinner は無冠詞が基本)

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I eat breakfast to my family.
common_errors_ja.correct: I have breakfast with my family.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 誰かと一緒に食事をとる場合は with を使う。to は不適切

confusables.similarity_ja: 宿題に関する表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: do は宿題を行う行為全体、finish は宿題を終える完了状態
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 取り組みなら do、完了なら finish
confusables.example_en: I need to finish my homework before dinner.

confusables.similarity_ja: 宿題に関する動作
confusables.key_difference_ja: do は自宅などで解く行為、submit は提出する行為
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 解く場合は do、提出する場合は submit(またはhand in)
confusables.example_en: Please submit your homework by Friday.

confusables.similarity_ja: 学習に関する動作で似ている
confusables.key_difference_ja: do homework は宿題を解く、study for a test はテスト勉強
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 宿題そのものなら do homework、テスト対策なら study
confusables.example_en: I have to study for a math test tomorrow.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I make my homework every day.
common_errors_ja.correct: I do my homework every day.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 宿題は make(作る)ではなく do(行う)を使う。「宿題を作る」と直訳する誤り

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I did the homework of math.
common_errors_ja.correct: I did my math homework.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 教科を示すときは前置詞 of ではなく、名詞を形容詞的に使う(math homework)

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I do homeworks every night.
common_errors_ja.correct: I do homework every night.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: homework は不可算名詞なので複数形 -s を付けない

confusables.similarity_ja: 風邪の話で似ている
confusables.key_difference_ja: catch は感染の瞬間(なった時)、have は既にひいている状態
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 発症の瞬間・過去形で catch、現在ひいている状態は have
confusables.example_en: I have a cold now, so I'm staying home.

confusables.similarity_ja: 風邪をひく意味でよく似ている
confusables.key_difference_ja: get はよりカジュアル・口語的、catch は少しフォーマル寄り
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 会話ではどちらも自然、書き言葉では catch がやや無難
confusables.example_en: I got a cold last weekend and stayed in bed.

confusables.similarity_ja: 同じ catch を使う病気表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: cold は風邪、flu はインフルエンザで別の病気
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 軽い風邪症状なら cold、高熱・全身症状なら flu
confusables.example_en: He caught the flu at school last week.

confusables.similarity_ja: cold を含むが意味が違う
confusables.key_difference_ja: feel cold は「寒く感じる」で体感温度、病気ではない
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 体感温度は feel cold、病気は catch a cold
confusables.example_en: Close the window, I feel cold.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I catched a cold yesterday.
common_errors_ja.correct: I caught a cold yesterday.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: catch の過去形は caught で不規則変化。catched は誤り

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I took a cold from my brother.
common_errors_ja.correct: I caught a cold from my brother.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 英語では「風邪をひく」を take で表現しない。必ず catch(または get)を使う

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I caught cold last week.
common_errors_ja.correct: I caught a cold last week.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 現代英語では不定冠詞 a が必要(catch cold は古風・文語的で日常会話ではまれ)

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I caught a cold from the weather.
common_errors_ja.correct: I caught a cold because of the cold weather.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: from の後は通常「人・場所」など感染源が来る。天気が原因の場合は because of

confusables.similarity_ja: シャワーを浴びる意味で同じ
confusables.key_difference_ja: have a shower は英国英語で一般的、take a shower は米国英語で一般的
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 米国英語圏なら take、英国英語圏なら have、どちらも通じる
confusables.example_en: I usually have a shower before bed.

confusables.similarity_ja: 同じ take + 入浴表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: shower は立ってシャワーを浴びる、bath は浴槽に浸かる
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 浴槽に浸かる場合は bath、シャワーだけなら shower
confusables.example_en: After a long day, I love to take a bath.

confusables.similarity_ja: シャワーに関する動作
confusables.key_difference_ja: get in the shower は「シャワーを開始する」、take a shower は「シャワーを浴びる行為全体」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 行為全体は take、始める瞬間は get in
confusables.example_en: I'll call you back — I'm about to get in the shower.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I make a shower every morning.
common_errors_ja.correct: I take a shower every morning.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: シャワーを浴びる意味では make ではなく take(または have)を使う

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I take a shower bath.
common_errors_ja.correct: I take a shower.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: shower と bath を同時に使うことはない。どちらか一方を選ぶ

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I took shower after running.
common_errors_ja.correct: I took a shower after running.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 通常は不定冠詞 a が必要(take a shower の a を落とさない)

confusables.similarity_ja: 買い物をする意味で似ている
confusables.key_difference_ja: go shopping は「買い物のために外出する」、do the shopping は「日常の必需品の買い物をこなす」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 外出行動なら go shopping、日常タスクなら do the shopping
confusables.example_en: My mother does the shopping every Saturday.

confusables.similarity_ja: 買い物に関連する外出
confusables.key_difference_ja: go to the store は特定の店に行くこと、go shopping は「買い物という活動」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 目的地が明確なら go to the store、活動を伝えるなら go shopping
confusables.example_en: I need to go to the store to buy some milk.

confusables.similarity_ja: shopping を含む表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: window shopping は「見るだけで買わない」、shopping は「実際に買う」ことを含む
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 買わないで見るだけなら window shopping、買う場合は shopping
confusables.example_en: We went window shopping downtown yesterday.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I go to shopping every Sunday.
common_errors_ja.correct: I go shopping every Sunday.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: go + 動詞ing の形では前置詞 to を入れない(go swimming, go fishing と同じ)

common_errors_ja.incorrect: Let's go for shopping this weekend.
common_errors_ja.correct: Let's go shopping this weekend.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: go shopping の間に前置詞 for は不要。go + Ving は前置詞なしが基本形

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I went shopped yesterday.
common_errors_ja.correct: I went shopping yesterday.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: go の後は動詞ing 形(現在分詞)を使う。過去形にしない

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I go shopping to the mall.
common_errors_ja.correct: I go shopping at the mall.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 場所を示すときは at を使うのが自然。to だと「モールに向かって買い物する」で不自然

confusables.similarity_ja: 音楽と動詞の組み合わせ
confusables.key_difference_ja: hear は「聞こえる」(受動的)、listen は「聴く」(能動的・意識的)
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 意識せず耳に入る場合は hear、集中して聴く場合は listen
confusables.example_en: I hear music from the next room.

confusables.similarity_ja: 音楽関連の動詞
confusables.key_difference_ja: play music は「音楽を演奏する / 再生する」、listen to music は「聴く」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 演奏・再生する側なら play、聴く側なら listen to
confusables.example_en: She plays music on her guitar every evening.

confusables.similarity_ja: 音楽を楽しむ表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: enjoy は感情(楽しむ)、listen to は行為(聴く)
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 感覚を伝えるなら enjoy、動作を伝えるなら listen to
confusables.example_en: I really enjoy classical music in the evening.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I listen music every day.
common_errors_ja.correct: I listen to music every day.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: listen は自動詞なので、目的語の前に必ず前置詞 to が必要

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I hear to music while I study.
common_errors_ja.correct: I listen to music while I study.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: hear には to を付けない。能動的に聴く意味なら listen to を使う

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I listen to music of Mozart.
common_errors_ja.correct: I listen to Mozart's music. / I listen to music by Mozart.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 作曲者を示すときは of ではなく所有格 's か前置詞 by を使う

confusables.similarity_ja: 対比を導く意味で同じ
confusables.key_difference_ja: but は口語・書き言葉両方でカジュアル、however は書き言葉や formal な文脈
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 話し言葉やカジュアルな文章なら but、レポートやビジネス文書なら however
confusables.example_en: It's raining, but we'll still go out.

confusables.similarity_ja: 対比を導ける
confusables.key_difference_ja: though は文末で「〜だけどね」の informal、however は文頭の formal
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 口語で軽く付け加えるなら though、書き言葉で対比を示すなら however
confusables.example_en: It's cold today. It'll warm up tomorrow, though.

confusables.similarity_ja: 対比を導く formal な副詞
confusables.key_difference_ja: nevertheless は「それにもかかわらず」の強い対比、however はより中立的な対比
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 強い予想外なら nevertheless、単純な対比なら however
confusables.example_en: The task was difficult; nevertheless, they finished on time.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: But however, he was late.
common_errors_ja.correct: However, he was late.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: but と however は両方とも対比を導くので重複。どちらか一方だけ使う

common_errors_ja.incorrect: However he tried, he failed.
common_errors_ja.correct: Although he tried, he failed. / However hard he tried, he failed.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 節を導入するときは although。however は独立文の前で使う対比副詞

common_errors_ja.incorrect: It was rainy however cold.
common_errors_ja.correct: It was rainy but not cold.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: however を単純な「〜だけど」で使わない。文と文の対比副詞として使う

confusables.similarity_ja: black と white を含む表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: black and white は「白黒両方(明確)」、black or white は「白か黒のどちらか(選択)」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 明確・両方を含むなら and、どちらか選ぶなら or
confusables.example_en: You can order the shirt in black or white.

confusables.similarity_ja: 写真・映像の対比
confusables.key_difference_ja: in color は「カラーで」、in black and white は「白黒で」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: カラー写真は in color、白黒写真は in black and white
confusables.example_en: The old movie was filmed in color.

confusables.similarity_ja: 色を使った比喩表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: gray area は「はっきりしない領域」、black and white は「明確な区別」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 曖昧な部分は gray area、明確な部分は black and white
confusables.example_en: There's a gray area in the rules that needs clarification.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: The photo is white and black.
common_errors_ja.correct: The photo is black and white.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 英語では black and white の順序が固定。white and black は不自然でほぼ言わない

common_errors_ja.incorrect: It's black or white to me.
common_errors_ja.correct: It's black and white to me.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 「白黒明確」の意味では and。or だと「白か黒のどちらか」で意味が変わる

common_errors_ja.incorrect: I like blacks and whites.
common_errors_ja.correct: I like black and white.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 色を表す形容詞的用法では複数形 -s を付けない

confusables.similarity_ja: 同じ of course を含む対の表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: of course は「もちろん(肯定)」、of course not は「もちろん違う(否定)」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 同意なら of course、否定なら of course not
confusables.example_en: Do you mind? Of course not, go ahead.

confusables.similarity_ja: 同意を表す表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: certainly はよりフォーマルで「確かに」、of course は「当然」でカジュアル寄り
confusables.correct_usage_ja: ビジネスや接客なら certainly、日常なら of course
confusables.example_en: Certainly, sir, I'll bring that right away.

confusables.similarity_ja: 同意を表す表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: sure はカジュアルで「うん・いいよ」、of course は「もちろん・当然」
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 気軽な返事なら sure、当然性を示すなら of course
confusables.example_en: Can I borrow your pen? Sure, here you go.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: Of the course, I agree.
common_errors_ja.correct: Of course, I agree.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: of course は固定表現で、定冠詞 the を入れない

common_errors_ja.incorrect: By course, you can come.
common_errors_ja.correct: Of course, you can come.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 「もちろん」の意味では必ず of course。by course は英語として不自然

common_errors_ja.incorrect: It is of course truely.
common_errors_ja.correct: It is, of course, true. / Of course, it is true.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 文中に挿入する場合はカンマで区切る。副詞 truely も不要

confusables.similarity_ja: 例示を導く意味で同じ
confusables.key_difference_ja: for instance は for example よりわずかにフォーマル、意味はほぼ同じ
confusables.correct_usage_ja: どちらも使えるが、書き言葉ではやや for instance が上品
confusables.example_en: There are many options. For instance, you could take the train.

confusables.similarity_ja: 例を示す表現
confusables.key_difference_ja: for example は独立した挿入句(文で使う)、such as は名詞の直後に続ける
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 文を分けるなら for example、名詞リストなら such as
confusables.example_en: Fruits such as apples and bananas are healthy.

confusables.similarity_ja: 例示を導くラテン語由来の略記
confusables.key_difference_ja: e.g. は書き言葉の略記(括弧内などに使う)、for example は文中に自然に使える
confusables.correct_usage_ja: 書き言葉の技術文書などなら e.g.、口語や一般文なら for example
confusables.example_en: Bring a snack (e.g., cookies or fruit) to the meeting.

common_errors_ja.incorrect: For a example, apples are red.
common_errors_ja.correct: For example, apples are red.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: for example は固定表現で、不定冠詞 a を入れない

common_errors_ja.incorrect: As example, apples are red.
common_errors_ja.correct: For example, apples are red.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: 「例えば」の意味では必ず for example。as example は英語として不自然

common_errors_ja.incorrect: For example fruits such as apples are healthy.
common_errors_ja.correct: Fruits, for example apples, are healthy. / Fruits such as apples are healthy.
common_errors_ja.why_ja: for example と such as は同時に使わない。どちらか一方を選び、for example はカンマで区切る
